title: [ExportVerilog] multi-used readinout ops cause extraneous temporaries
body:
This:

```
 hw.module @Thing(%auto_in_ar_bits_id : i2) -> (%aa: i3, %bb: i3){
    %119 = sv.reg  : !hw.inout<i3>
    %120 = sv.reg  : !hw.inout<i3>
    %121 = sv.reg  : !hw.inout<i3>
    %122 = sv.reg  : !hw.inout<i3>
    %123 = sv.read_inout %120 : !hw.inout<i3>
    %124 = sv.read_inout %119 : !hw.inout<i3>
    %125 = sv.read_inout %121 : !hw.inout<i3>
    %126 = sv.read_inout %122 : !hw.inout<i3>
    %127 = hw.array_create %124, %123, %125, %126 : i3
    %128 = hw.array_get %127[%auto_in_ar_bits_id] : !hw.array<4xi3>

    %xx = comb.add %123, %124 : i3
   hw.output %128, %xx : i3, i3
}
```

Codegens into this verilog:

```
module Thing(
  input  [1:0] auto_in_ar_bits_id,
  output [2:0] aa, bb);

  reg [2:0] _T;	// y.mlir:2:12
  reg [2:0] _T_0;	// y.mlir:3:12
  reg [2:0] _T_1;	// y.mlir:4:12
  reg [2:0] _T_2;	// y.mlir:5:12

  wire [2:0] _T_3 = _T_0;	// y.mlir:6:12
  wire [2:0] _T_4 = _T;	// y.mlir:7:12
  assign aa = ({{_T_4}, {_T_3}, {_T_1}, {_T_2}})[auto_in_ar_bits_id];	// y.mlir:8:12, :9:12, :10:12, :11:12, :14:4
  assign bb = _T_3 + _T_4;	// y.mlir:13:11, :14:4
endmodule
```

We don't need the extra _T_4/_T_3 wires.
